message:
test(share,cli): add golden-artifact tests for robot CLI output and share bundle manifest

Add a golden-artifact regression regime that pins the shape of the
CLI robot output (JSON / TOON / Markdown) and the share-bundle
manifest JSON plus its companion file_manifest.sha256 against
frozen known-good files under tests/golden/. Either mismatch fails
the test; running with `UPDATE_GOLDENS=1` rewrites the fixtures, so
intentional output changes are a one-command regenerate-and-diff.

New tests/ directory
- tests/golden/cli/robot/status/{json.json, toon.toon} — status
  output in both machine formats.
- tests/golden/cli/robot/message/md.md — compose message rendering.
- tests/golden/cli/robot/thread/md.md — thread rendering.
- tests/golden/share/bundle/manifest_standard.json — canonical
  bundle manifest shape.
- tests/golden/share/bundle/file_manifest.sha256 — companion
  per-file checksum list.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/tests/robot_golden_snapshots.rs (new)
- `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`
- Helper repo_root() walks two levels up from CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR to
  land on the workspace root so golden fixtures live in one place.
- `update_goldens_requested()` reads `UPDATE_GOLDENS` (or
  `UPDATE_GOLDEN`) env var as the regenerate toggle.
- `assert_golden(rel_path, actual)` writes fixtures when
  regenerating, otherwise diffs byte-for-byte and fails the test on
  mismatch with a path-aware message.
- Tests cover RobotEnvelope::format_output (JSON + TOON) for Status,
  and format_output_md for Message and Thread.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-share/src/bundle.rs
- In-crate `mod tests` gains the same `repo_root / update_goldens /
  assert_repo_golden` helpers (local copy so the share crate doesn't
  take a test-only dep on the CLI crate) plus a test that pins
  manifest_standard.json byte-for-byte.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-share/src/lib.rs
- Pure cargo fmt rewrap of one `manifest_root.get("project_scope")`
  chain that was on a too-long line. No behaviour change.

The regime matches the established "fresh eyes review" pattern: CI
fails if a code change silently drifts the wire shape of any of
these user-facing outputs.

@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+## Message #101 | Thread: br-robot-golden (2 of 3)
+
+**From:** BlueLake (claude-code, opus-4.6)  
+**To:** RedFox  
+**Subject:** Robot golden fixture  
+**Importance:** high | **Ack:** required  
+**Sent:** 2026-01-02T03:02:00Z (2m)
+
+---
+
+Fixture body used to catch markdown drift.
+
+**Attachments:** 1
+- audit-notes.txt (128 B, text/plain)
+
+**← Previous:** 100
+**→ Next:** 102

+    }
+  ],
+  "top_threads": [
+    {
+      "id": "br-robot-golden",
+      "subject": "Freeze robot output",
+      "participants": 2,
+      "messages": 3,
+      "last_activity": "2026-01-02T03:04:00Z"
+    }
+  ]
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,23 @@
+_meta:
+  command: robot status
+  timestamp: "2026-01-02T03:04:05Z"
+  format: toon
+  version: "1.0"
+  project: /workspace/project-alpha
+  agent: RedFox
+_alerts[1]{severity,summary,action}:
+  warn,One reservation expires soon,am robot reservations --expiring=30
+_actions[1]: am robot inbox --urgent
+health: ok
+unread: 2
+urgent: 1
+ack_required: 1
+ack_overdue: 0
+active_reservations: 1
+reservations_expiring_soon: 0
+active_agents: 2
+recent_messages: 3
+my_reservations[1]{agent,path,exclusive,remaining_seconds,remaining,granted_at}:
+  RedFox,crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/**,true,3600,1h,"2026-01-02T03:00:00Z"
+top_threads[1]{id,subject,participants,messages,last_activity}:
+  br-robot-golden,Freeze robot output,2,3,"2026-01-02T03:04:00Z"

@@ -0,0 +1,16 @@
+# Thread: robot thread br-robot-golden
+
+## [1] BlueLake → RedFox (3m)
+**Start robot golden thread**
+
+Opening note for the golden thread.
+
+---
+
+## [2] RedFox → BlueLake (1m)
+**Re: Start robot golden thread**
+
+Reply body that should remain stable.
+
+---
+
